text: <@U0BDGG2FRHB> Task 1: Install and configure Laravel Sanctum by running 'php artisan vendor:publish --provider="Laravel\Sanctum\SanctumServiceProvider"' and 'php artisan migrate'. text:
<@U0BDGG2FRHB> Task 1: TASK 1 — Database Migrations & Models (Multi-tenant core)

Create the foundational database schema and Eloquent models for multi-tenancy.

**Files to create:**
1. `database/migrations/YYYY_MM_DD_000001_create_organizations_table.php`:
   - Columns: `id` (uuid or bigIncrements), `name` (string), `slug` (string, unique), `timestamps`, `softDeletes()`.
2. `database/migrations/YYYY_MM_DD_000002_create_users_table.php`:
   - Columns: `id`, `organization_id` (foreign key to `organizations.id`, cascadeOnDelete), `name`, `email` (unique), `email_verified_at`, `password`, `role` (enum: `admin`, `agent`, `customer` — default `customer`), `remember_token`, `timestamps`, `softDeletes()`.
   - Add unique constraint on `email` and index on `organization_id`.
3. `app/Models/Organization.php` — fillable: `name`, `slug`. Has hasMany `User`.
4. `app/Models/User.php` — update to include `organization_id` and `role` in fillable. Has belongsTo `Organization`. Append `role` to hidden/visible as needed. Remove the existing `database/migrations/0001_01_01_000000_create_users_table.php` if it conflicts, or merge columns into it.

**Acceptance:** Running `php artisan migrate:fresh` succeeds with no errors. Both tables exist with correct columns and constraints.

text:
<@U0BDGG2FRHB> Task 1: Task 1 — Migrations & Models:

1. Create `database/migrations/YYYY_MM_DD_HHMMSS_create_organizations_table.php` with columns: `id` (ulid/uuid), `name` (string), `slug` (string, unique), `timestamps`, `softDeletes()`.
2. Create `database/migrations/YYYY_MM_DD_HHMMSS_create_users_table.php` with columns: `id` (ulid/uuid), `organization_id` (foreign → `organizations.id`, cascadeOnDelete), `name` (string), `email` (string, unique), `email_verified_at` (timestamp, nullable), `password` (string), `role` (enum: `admin`,`agent`,`customer`, default `customer`), `remember_token`, `timestamps`, `softDeletes()`.
3. Create `app/Models/Organization.php` — fillable `['name','slug']`, uses `HasFactory`, `SoftDeletes`.
4. Create `app/Models/User.php` — fillable `['organization_id','name','email','password','role']`, hidden `['password','remember_token']`, casts `['email_verified_at'=>'datetime','password'=>'hashed']`, uses `HasFactory`, `Notifiable`, `HasApiTokens`, `SoftDeletes`; add `organization()` belongsTo and `organization()` relationship.
5. Register the `Organization` model in any existing map/config if applicable.
6. Run `php artisan migrate` (in the local DB) and confirm both tables exist.

Deliverable: Two new migration files + two new model classes committed; `php artisan migrate` runs clean.

Notify in #agent-coder on completion with commit hash. Delegate to <@U0BDGG2FRHB>.
